methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

private.name: Stark Defence

private.name: Vatn Systems

data.narrative:
Tytan Technologies is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised €30M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of three same-sector, same-stage private peers: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M). This peer set sits within a broader landscape of ten private companies and two public comparables in the Defense & Security sector (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS).

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given available data, yields an implied valuation of $300M. The three-company peer sample produces a range from $150M (minimum) to $500M (maximum), with a median of $300M. This represents the primary quantitative reference point for the company at this stage.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $255M to $345M, with a median of $300M. Key uncertainties include the limited sample size of directly comparable companies and the substantial spread within the peer range ($150M–$500M), which reflects material variance in company-specific factors within the same sector and stage. Additional methods (revenue multiples, venture capital method) could not be applied given data constraints. The $300M median should be treated as the point estimate pending further operational and financial detail.
